channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk's PAC is running ads touting Trump's accomplishments
item.description: The political action committee that Elon Musk funded to the tune of more than $200 million on behalf of President Donald Trump launched a television advertisement this week lauding the first weeks of the administration in which the Tesla CEO is serving.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump slashed teacher training, citing DEI. Educators say the grants fought staff shortages
item.description: Trump administration cuts to teacher training grants are putting a strain on rural school systems, which have relied on the money to help address teacher shortages.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man threatens to assassinate Trump, destroy New York with missiles in 911 calls: report
item.description: Justin Blaxton, 34, allegedly threatened to assassinate President Donald Trump and launch missiles to destroy New York in a series of 911 calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roy Ayers,'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect’s parents are no-shows for accused killer son's court appearance
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother and a family cat allegedly told police he had a "fit of madness" when they responded to the scene of the crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers and government officials in a broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Twelve Chinese nationals — including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company — have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAKE KANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Migrants clone Border Patrol vehicles in bid to bypass Trump's crackdown
item.description: Some human smugglers have turned to cloning Border Patrol vehicles to bypass President Trump's crackdown on illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 3 skiers buried in Alaska avalanche, officials say
item.description: An avalanche in the Alaska backcountry swept away and buried three skiers who were believed to be dead under a pile of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: By MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Detroit children in a van died from carbon monoxide not excessive cold, autopsies show
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two Detroit children who recently died in a van in below-freezing temperatures were victims of carbon monoxide, not exposure to cold weather, authorities said Wednesday. Police had said that Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How New Orleans cleans up the waves of trash left behind after Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people take to the streets of New Orleans to celebrate Mardi Gras with parades and partying, leaving behind an avalanche of waste.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fiancée of Faster Pussycat singer dies after going overboard on '80s-themed cruise, family says
item.description: Kimberly Burch, the fiancée of an '80s rock star, has died after going overboard an '80s-themed cruise, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KRON4

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman's dog misidentified as other mysteries swirl around actor's death
item.description: Authorities misidentified a deceased dog while investigating the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and his wife, pianist Betsy Arakawa, according to a pet care specialist.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ash Wednesday: What you should know about the start of the season
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) – What is Ash Wednesday? Ash Wednesday 2025, which falls on March 5 this year, marks the official beginning of the Christian season of Lent, which culminates with Easter on April 20, 2025. The 46-day season is meant for penitence, sacrifice, and reflection.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Los Angeles fire chief fails to get job back after ouster following historic deadly wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The former Los Angeles fire chief who was ousted by Mayor Karen Bass after the most destructive wildfire in city history failed to win back her job Tuesday, falling short with her argument that her dismissal was based on false accusations about her conduct and decision-making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, Fox 5

item.title: More endangered Mexican gray wolves are roaming the southwestern US, annual survey shows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It is a ritual that takes place every winter — federal and state wildlife managers use remote cameras, scat collection, radio telemetry devices and helicopters to count Mexican gray wolves that are roaming mountain ranges in parts of New Mexico and Arizona.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
